quick_facts.label: Cadre and status
quick_facts.value: Senior Technical Assistant-B (STA-B) is advertised under the Defence Research & Development Organisation Technical Cadre (DRTC) as a Group ‘B’, Non-Gazetted, Non-Ministerial post.

quick_facts.label: Recruiting authority
quick_facts.value: Direct recruitment of DRDO Senior Technical Assistants is managed by the Centre for Personnel Talent Management (CEPTAM).

quick_facts.label: Entry qualification context
quick_facts.value: In the CEPTAM-11 notification, eligibility is post-code-specific and requires the prescribed B.Sc. or diploma qualification. The notice says candidates holding higher qualifications such as B.Tech. or B.E. on the crucial eligibility date are not considered for that recruitment; applicants must always check the notification governing the particular cycle.

quick_facts.label: Selection context
quick_facts.value: For STA-B in CEPTAM-11, Tier-I CBT is a screening test and Tier-II includes a subject-specific test used for provisional selection. The notification states that the selection mode may change with organisational requirements or Government of India policy.

quick_facts.label: Pay context
quick_facts.value: The CEPTAM-11 advertisement lists STA-B at 7th CPC Pay Matrix Level 6, ₹35,400–₹1,12,400 basic pay, plus allowances and benefits under applicable Government of India rules. This is dated recruitment-notice context, not a guarantee of take-home pay.

sections.heading: Role in DRDO technical R&D work
paragraphs: STA-B is part of DRDO’s technical cadre rather than the scientist cadre. DRDO’s current recruitment material places the post in the Defence Research & Development Organisation Technical Cadre and recruits it against discipline-specific post codes, such as engineering and science disciplines. In practice, the technical contribution is therefore linked to the allocated discipline and laboratory or establishment rather than to one uniform job description for every STA-B appointment.

DRDO describes its organisational work as scientific research, design, development, testing and evaluation of defence systems, subsystems, devices and products. In that setting, an STA-B role is best understood as technical support work within the assigned R&D activity, under the work arrangements of the receiving lab or unit.
bullets: The precise discipline is determined by the advertised post code and its stated educational qualification.
The receiving DRDO lab, establishment or unit issues the appointment after nomination and document verification.
Applicants should not assume that the same day-to-day tasks apply across computer, electronics, mechanical, chemistry, agriculture or other advertised disciplines.

sections.heading: Direct-entry route through CEPTAM
paragraphs: The principal route is to apply when CEPTAM publishes an STA-B recruitment advertisement for a matching post code. CEPTAM handles advertisements, application scrutiny, computer-based tests, selection processes and recommendation of final select lists for Senior Technical Assistant recruitment.

For CEPTAM-11, the STA-B process used two computer-based tiers. Tier-I was a common screening examination covering general science, English, quantitative aptitude/general mathematics, and general intelligence and reasoning. Tier-II included a personality test and a subject-specific test; the notification specifies that the Tier-II merit list is based on the subject-specific part, not personality-test performance.

Eligibility must be read against the exact recruitment notice. CEPTAM-11 required the relevant B.Sc. or diploma listed for the post code, required that qualification by its stated crucial date, and expressly excluded candidates who already possessed specified higher qualifications including B.Tech. and B.E. Recruitment rules, post codes, vacancies, age conditions and selection arrangements can change in later notifications.
bullets: Match the diploma or B.Sc. subject exactly to an advertised STA-B post code.
Use only the official DRDO/CEPTAM notice for eligibility, dates, fees, reservation and examination instructions.
Selection is provisional until eligibility checks, document verification, medical fitness where required, and appointment formalities are completed.

sections.heading: Skills indicated by the recruitment process
paragraphs: The current CEPTAM-11 pattern indicates two complementary preparation areas: broad aptitude for Tier-I and technical command of the discipline assigned to the STA-B post code for Tier-II. Tier-I assesses general science, English, quantitative aptitude/general mathematics, and reasoning; Tier-II assesses the specific subject of the post code.

Because appointments are discipline-coded, candidates should build practical and theoretical capability in their qualifying diploma or B.Sc. domain, then prepare against the official syllabus released for that recruitment. This is especially important because provisional selection is based on Tier-II merit after eligibility scrutiny.
bullets: Technical knowledge in the exact advertised discipline or subject.
General science, English, quantitative aptitude/general mathematics, and reasoning for the screening CBT.
Careful document readiness: qualification evidence must establish that the prescribed requirement was held by the notification’s crucial eligibility date.

sections.heading: Posting, mobility and employment setting
paragraphs: Selected candidates are nominated to a DRDO laboratory, establishment or unit against the vacancies projected for the advertised post code. CEPTAM-11 states that nomination considers merit and the candidate’s submitted station preferences, but DRDO retains the right to post candidates anywhere in India according to organisational requirements.

The CEPTAM-11 notice lists postings across multiple locations and states that selected candidates have all-India service liability, including field locations and remote areas when required. It also states that initial appointment is on two years’ probation and that staff are governed by Central Government service rules.
bullets: Expect a lab, establishment or unit environment rather than a single centrally fixed workplace.
Station preference is relevant but is not an entitlement to a particular location.
The notice describes residential quarters, amenities and sports facilities at most laboratories or establishments; availability will vary by location.

sections.heading: Career progression and development
paragraphs: The CEPTAM-11 advertisement states that DRTC posts are covered by a merit-based Limited Flexible Complementing Scheme (LFCS) for promotion to the next higher grade. It does not, by itself, establish an automatic promotion or a universal timetable; employees should rely on the applicable DRTC rules and departmental instructions for current criteria.

DRDO also states that it emphasises human-resource development and offers training opportunities at institutions including IITs, IISc Bengaluru and DIAT Pune, as well as opportunities for specific training assignments. Availability and eligibility are organisational decisions, so these should be treated as possible development opportunities rather than entitlements for every STA-B employee.
bullets: Promotion is described as merit-based under the DRTC LFCS.
Check current departmental rules for qualifying service, assessment and education requirements before planning a move to a higher technical grade.
Training possibilities can support technical development after joining, subject to DRDO policy and organisational needs.

sections.heading: Pay and benefits: dated recruitment context
paragraphs: For the CEPTAM-11 recruitment cycle, STA-B was placed at Pay Matrix Level 6 under the 7th Central Pay Commission: ₹35,400–₹1,12,400 basic pay. The same notice says recruited staff are eligible for applicable dearness allowance, house-rent allowance, transport allowance, children’s education allowance, leave travel concession, medical facilities, CSD facility and other allowances or advances under extant Government of India rules.

Actual monthly take-home pay varies with posting location, prevailing allowance rates, pension choices and deductions. CEPTAM-11 also states that recruited candidates are covered under the National Pension System or Unified Pension Scheme, as applicable under then-extant Government of India rules. Future recruitment notices and government pay or allowance rules may differ.
bullets: Treat the quoted pay range as basic-pay matrix context from CEPTAM-11, not as a current net-salary estimate.
Verify the recruitment notice and Central Government rules in force when applying or joining.
Benefits and pension coverage are subject to applicable government rules and individual circumstances.
